title: Recognizing Russian actions in Ukraine as a genocide.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This resolution says that Russia is committing genocide against the people of Ukraine. The House of Representatives strongly condemns Russia for these terrible crimes against Ukrainian civilians. The resolution calls for the United States to work with its allies to help Ukraine and stop Russia from continuing these acts of genocide. It also supports international courts that are investigating these crimes so that Russian leaders and soldiers can be held responsible for what they have done.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
subjects: War crimes, genocide, crimes against humanity

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes Russian actions in Ukraine as genocide.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: Russia started an illegal, planned, and brutal war against Ukraine that includes widespread and systematic crimes against Ukrainian people. The Genocide Convention is an international agreement from nineteen forty-eight that defines genocide as acts committed with the intent to destroy a national, ethnic, racial, or religious group. These acts include killing group members, causing serious harm to group members, creating living conditions meant to destroy the group, preventing births within the group, and forcibly moving children from the group to another group. There is strong evidence that Russian forces, under orders from Russian political leaders, have committed actions that meet one or more of these genocide definitions. These actions include killing Ukrainian people through mass murders of civilians and targeting homes, schools, hospitals, and other civilian areas. Russian forces have caused serious harm by attacking civilians, striking evacuation routes, and using widespread sexual violence against Ukrainian civilians including women, children, and men. Russia has created conditions meant to destroy Ukrainian people by destroying villages, towns, and cities, leaving people without food, water, shelter, electricity, and other basic needs. They have destroyed farmland and agricultural equipment, placed landmines across thousands of acres of useable fields, and blocked humanitarian food aid. Russia has tried to prevent Ukrainian births by targeting maternity hospitals and medical facilities and attacking residential areas and evacuation routes to deprive Ukrainians of safe places and conditions needed for raising children. Russia has forcibly moved millions of Ukrainian civilians to Russia or Russian-controlled areas, including hundreds of thousands of children. Russian leaders have shown their intent through official statements denying that Ukraine is a real nation, including President Putin's false claims that Ukraine is part of Russia with no historical basis for independence. Some Russian soldiers accused of war crimes were given medals by President Putin. Russian state media published an article outlining plans to destroy Ukraine as a nation. The Genocide Convention states that genocide is a crime under international law that must be prevented and punished whether it happens during peace or war. While more documentation may be needed to punish those responsible, the substantial evidence already collected, combined with statements showing intent, requires urgent action to prevent further acts of genocide.

sections.title: Resolution Actions
sections.text: The House of Representatives condemns the Russian Federation for committing acts of genocide against the Ukrainian people. The House calls on the United States, working with North Atlantic Treaty Organization (a military alliance) and European Union allies, to take steps to support the Ukrainian Government in preventing further acts of Russian genocide against Ukrainian people. The House supports tribunals (special courts) and international criminal investigations to hold Russian political leaders and military personnel accountable for a war of aggression, war crimes, crimes against humanity, and genocide.
